statement.en: A truncated soil phage catechol 1,2-dioxygenase illustrates how viruses preserve and disseminate auxiliary catalytic functions in the soil microbiome
statement.summary: This repository’s source data are archived on Zenodo to provide a permanent, citable record with a DOI. The Zenodo record is the canonical citation for the dataset used in the manuscript, while the GitHub repository specified in the Code Availability section hosts the analysis code and documentation.

proposer: Ruonan Wu, Garry W. Buchko, Ryan McClure et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint/provisional dataset archive (2026 date, Zenodo-hosted data with DOI) describing novel viral enzyme function. The scientific claim (viral auxiliary metabolic genes for catechol degradation) is empirically testable but not yet peer-reviewed. Classification as FLOWING reflects: (1) pending validation status, (2) data/code availability enabling independent verification, (3) claim specificity sufficient for refutation or confirmation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: ウイルスが絶滅した宿主の触媒機能を、時間と共に減衰しながらも永遠に運ぶ。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 二つの宿主の遺産が、ウイルスの中心で触媒のような弧を描く。
